message:
test(fuzz): add ObjectTransmissionInfo raptorq + serde round-trip fuzz target (resolves e2wq6)

ObjectTransmissionInfo (symbol_store.rs:69-115) is the serializable
wrapper around raptorq's ObjectTransmissionInformation — the symbol-
stream "header" telling decoders how to interpret a payload
(transfer_length, symbol_size, source_blocks, sub_blocks, alignment,
optional payload_hash). A regression that swapped field positions in
from_oti/to_oti or dropped payload_hash through serde would silently
cause sender/receiver decoders to diverge, breaking content addressing
for the affected object.

Existing fcp-store fuzz coverage does not probe this OTI bridge.

Six properties:
- OTI round-trip from_oti → wrapper → to_oti preserves every field
- field-position invariance: wrapper accessors agree 1:1 with raptorq's
  (regression that swapped source_blocks↔sub_blocks would still pass
  JSON schema but yield a wrong decoder config)
- JSON serde round-trip preserves all fields including optional
  payload_hash
- canonical-CBOR serde round-trip via fcp_cbor::CanonicalSerializer
- payload_hash optionality: with/without are distinct values that
  round-trip to themselves
- From<ObjectTransmissionInformation> agrees with from_oti

Once-gated regression anchor: known OTI (8192, 128, 1, 1, 8) with a
known payload_hash MUST round-trip byte-for-byte through raptorq, JSON,
and canonical CBOR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -518,5 +518,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_store_oti_roundtrip"
+path = "fuzz_targets/store_oti_roundtrip.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
